heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 04 January 2025 with a total accumulation of 36.8mm over 37.2 hours and falling at a rate of 4.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: There was a torrential downpour today where the rain reached a maximum intensity of 16.2mm per hour (ranked 4th in our records).  The storm lasted 6.9 hours and 9.36mm of precipitation fell during that time.  On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 27.0mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 37.2 hours on 04 January 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 36.8mm with a maximum intensity of 4.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

nearest_lightning.status: The closest strike happened on 13 May 2025, a distance of just 4.97 miles away.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 13 September 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest rainy periods recorded.  This rainy period is ranked 9th since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 09 September 2025 and has lasted 6 days.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (189°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1754 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.8°C), outside temperature (down 2.5°C), and inside temperature (down 1.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 8%) and rainfall (up 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 655 m), rainfall (down 0.1 mm), and light levels (up 5274 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1253 m), light levels (up 7218 lux), and rainfall (down 0.5 mm) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 655.3m lower than yesterday, 795.0m lower than the past week, 1252.6m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 845m to a maximum of 3460m, averaging 931.3m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1726.3m and 2180m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, very clammy and humid and near calm conditions. Light to moderate rain is forecast, clouds will occasionally block sunlight, and periods of sunshine are expected. Weather could become unsettled.
